OPEN for ALL

A Better Chance Scholarship
A Better Chance Foundation Inc
Overview
The A Better Chance Foundation Scholarship Program, in partnership with the Polytechnic University of
the Philippines (PUP), provides support to students from low-income families. Its goal is to help them
continue their education and achieve their goals.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Educational financial assistance per MOA
Eligibility
Minimum GPA: 2.00 (85%)
Financial Status: Must come from a financially disadvantaged family.
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Priority Programs
● Journalism
● Information Technology
● Office Management
● Finance Management
● Engineering
● Health Sciences
● HRM
Requirements:
1. Copy of latest academic records
2. PUP Certificate of Registration (COR)
3. Proof of Financial Status
4. Personal Data Sheet (PDS)
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information

3. Proof of Financial Status
4. Personal Data Sheet (PDS)
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.facebook.com/abetterchancefoundation2001/

Chinese Filipino Business Club Scholarship
Chinese Filipino Business Club, Inc.
Overview
Chinese-Filipino Business Club, Inc. (CFBCI) is one of the prominent non-profit organizations in the
Filipino-Chinese community with a membership of more than 300 individuals of the Sino-Filipino
community.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Educational financial assistance per MOA
Eligibility
Financial Status: Must come from a financially disadvantaged family.
Minimum GPA: 2.00 (85%)
Priority Programs
● Architecture
● Accounting
● Engineering
● Education (Major in Math & English)
Requirements:
1. Accomplished Application Form
2. Certified true copy of grades
3. If incoming college student, photocopy of college admission notice.
4. Copies of utility bills
5. Autobiography
6. Photos of inside and outside of residence
7. Sketch of home address.
8. Recommendation from Office of Student Affairs or High School Principal.
Exam Information:
Exam Type: None
Deadline
May 30, 2026

7. Sketch of home address.
8. Recommendation from Office of Student Affairs or High School Principal.
Exam Information:
Exam Type: None
Deadline
May 30, 2026
Submit the requirements to Room 119, OSFA, PUP Sta. Mesa, Manila.
More Information
https://cfbci.ph/

JAPRL Foundation Scholarship
JAPRL Foundation Inc.
Overview
non-profit organization that provides scholarship grants and learning opportunities to financially
challenged individuals
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Weekly transportation allowance, books, projects, and other school related fees.
Eligibility
Financial Status: Must come from a financially disadvantaged family.
Minimum GPA: 2.00 (85%)
Priority Programs
● Open to all Programs
Requirements:
1. Complete Grades
2. Registration Form
3. Application Form
4. Intake Interview Form
5. Current utility bill
6. OSFA Recommendation
7. Sketch of address
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.facebook.com/japrlfoundation.inc

Megaworld Foundation
Megaworld Foundation
Overview
The Megaworld Foundation Scholarship Program has been helping lots of students achieve their dreams
for a long time. They believe education can change lives, so they support 1,000 students in 76 schools in
Metro Manila and other places.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Semestral allowance, training and seminars, internship opportunities, and career opportunities.
Eligibility
Minimum GPA: General average of 85% with no grade lower than 80%
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 400,000
Must be resident near any Megaworld township or project
Priority Programs
● Accounting Technology 	Tourism Management
● Accounting Information System 	Supply Management
● Advertising and Public Relations Operations Management
● Architecture 	Management Accounting
● Business Administration 	Interior Design
● Business Economics 	Industrial Engineering

● Advertising and Public Relations Operations Management
● Architecture 	Management Accounting
● Business Administration 	Interior Design
● Business Economics 	Industrial Engineering
● Financial Management 	Hospitality Management
Requirements:
1. Current Registration/Enrollment Form
2. Complete Report of Grades
3. Barangay Certificate of Residency (Applicant)
4. Proof of Parents’ Financial Ability
5. Birth Certificate
6. House Photos
7. Photograph of applicant with family/guardian
8. Latest utility bills
9. Official School ID
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.facebook.com/officialmegaworldcorp
Incoming College

Andres P. Tamayo Sr. Scholarship
Andres P. Tamayo Sr. Foundation, Inc.
Overview
We believe in the youth’s vital role and contribution to our society. Thus, we are committed to support the
underserved youth nationwide to have a brighter future through the provision of college scholarship grants
and values formation training.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
The Scholarship Grant includes monthly allowance, other fees, and formations / trainings.
Eligibility
Minimum GPA: 2.00 (85%)
Nationality: Must be a Filipino citizen.
FInancial Status: Must be from indigent families as assessed by the DSWD
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Priority Programs
● All CHED recognized programs
Requirements:
1. Application Form w/ 2x2 Picture and Contact Number
2. Personal Letter of Intent
3. State University Entrance Exam Result
4. Grade 12 Report Card and Good Moral certificate.
5. Case Study Report from Municipality Social Worker

2. Personal Letter of Intent
3. State University Entrance Exam Result
4. Grade 12 Report Card and Good Moral certificate.
5. Case Study Report from Municipality Social Worker
6.Proof of Financial Status
Exam Information:
Exam Type: None
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://andresptamayosrfoundation.com/

SM Foundation College Scholarship
SM Foundation
Overview
The primary goal of the SM Foundation College Scholarship is to extend financial support and assistance
to deserving students facing economic challenges on their journey to higher education.
Partial Coverage | Private | ALL Strand
Coverage
Php 100,000.00 per academic year
Eligibility
● Must have a General average grade of at least 92% or its equivalent for Grade 12 – 1st semester.
● Must have a total household income of at most P250,000 per year.
● Must be a Filipino citizen.
● Private school applicants must hold a DepEd voucher.
● Private school applicants must have completed Grade 10 in public high schools.
Priority Programs
● BS in Computer Science 	BS in Computer Engineering
● BS in Accounting Information Systems 	BS in Mechanical Engineering
● BS in Information Technology 	BSBA in Management Accounting
● BS in Civil Engineering 	BS in Accountancy
● BS in Internal Auditing 	BS in Elementary Education

● BS in Information Technology 	BSBA in Management Accounting
● BS in Civil Engineering 	BS in Accountancy
● BS in Internal Auditing 	BS in Elementary Education
● BS Electronics Engineering 	BS in Electrical Engineering
● BSBA in Financial Management 	BS in Accounting Technology
● BS in Secondary Education major in Biology, Chemistry, General Sciences, Physics,
Mathematics, English
Requirements:
● Accomplished online application form:
○ Parent’s or Guardian’s Latest Income Tax Return Certificate of Indigency
● Latest Grade 12 report card
● Birth Certificate/PSA
● Latest 2×2 ID picture
● Sketch of home to the nearest SM Mall
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Examination, Interview, and Home Visit
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.sm-foundation.org/what_we_do/college-scholarship-program/

Security Bank External Scholarship
Security Bank Foundation, Inc.
Overview
The Security Bank Foundation External Scholarship provides financial aid to students who lack access to
traditional scholarships, ensuring equal opportunities for higher education.
Partial Coverage | Private | ALL Strand
Coverage
Php 100,000.00 per academic year
Eligibility
● Must be an incoming first-year college student in any of the 8 partner universities
● Must be a continuing college student (limited slots available in specific universities)
● Must have a General Weighted Average (GWA) of at least 93% or its equivalent
● Must have no subject grade below 86% or its equivalent
● Must have grades of 90% and above in High School (Grades 7–12) subjects aligned with their
college course
● Must not be a child of a Security Bank employee
● Must not have any other family member currently admitted to the scholarship within the second
degree of consanguinity or affinity

college course
● Must not be a child of a Security Bank employee
● Must not have any other family member currently admitted to the scholarship within the second
degree of consanguinity or affinity
● Must not have any existing scholarship except for school-provided discounts
Priority Programs
● Accountancy 	Information Systems
● Accounting Information System 	Computer Science
● Internal Auditing 	Data Science
● Marketing 	Statistics
● Financial Management 	Business Analytics
● Industrial Engineering 	Applied Mathematics
● Management Engineering
Requirements:
● Duly accomplished Online Application Form
● High School Grade 7-12 Transcript of Records
● Proof of Financial Status
● Proof of Admission / Acceptance Letter
● Birth Certificate and Portrait Photo
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.securitybank.com/foundation/sbfi-scholars-for-better-communities/
Existing College

AGAP Scholarship
Association of Government Accountants of the Philippines (AGAP)
Overview
AGAP scholarship program extends financial assistance to well deserving students who are taking up a
degree in accountancy.
Partial Coverage | Private | BSA
Coverage
Php. 40,000 per year
Eligibility
Minimum GPA: 2.50
Priority Program: 3rd or 4th year BS Accountancy Student
Nationality: Must be a Filipino citizen.
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 300,000
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Priority Programs
● BS Accountancy
Requirements:
1. Application Form w/ 2x2 Picture and Contact Number.
2. Personal Letter of Intent.
3.Proof of Financial Status.
Exam Information:
Exam Type: None
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://agap.org.ph/

Buddhist Compassion Relief Tzu Chi Foundation Philippines, Inc.
Buddhist Compassion Relief Tzu Chi Foundation Philippines, Inc.
Overview
The Tzu Chi Foundation Philippines is committed to supporting students who are determined to achieve
their academic goals despite financial challenges. Our Educational Assistance Program provides financial
aid to deserving students enrolled in partner State Universities and Colleges.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Monthly allowance, benefits, and other educational support
Eligibility
Minimum GPA: 2.25 (82%)
Residency: Metro Manila or Rizal
Year Level: Must be a current 1st year college student
Financial Status: Must come from a financially disadvantaged family.
Priority Programs
• Communication 	• Physical Therapy
• Journalism 	• Information Technology
• Education 	• Medical Technology
• Social Work 	• Radiology Technology
• Economics 	• Business Administration
• Agriculture 	• Engineering and Architecture

• Journalism 	• Information Technology
• Education 	• Medical Technology
• Social Work 	• Radiology Technology
• Economics 	• Business Administration
• Agriculture 	• Engineering and Architecture
• Accountancy 	• Industrial Technology
Requirements:
1. Latest report card
2. Latest registration form
3. 1x1 ID Picture and Family Picture
4. Autobiography
5. Pictures of Inside and Outside of House
6. Copy of utility bills and sketch map with landmarks
7. Barangay Certificate of Indigency
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview & Home Visitation
Deadline
April 21, 2026 Deadline of Submission
More Information
https://tzuchi.org.ph/apply

Cebuana Lhuiller Scholarship
Cebuanna Lhuillier Foundation, Inc.
Overview
Dedicated to creating better opportunities, Cebuana Lhuillier Foundation Inc. serves as the philanthropic
arm of the PJ Lhuillier Group of Companies. The foundation believes in empowering people through
education and prioritizes inclusivity in its programs.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Up to Php. 50,000 fixed stipend.
Eligibility
Nationality: Must be a Filipino citizen.
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 350,000
Minimum GPA: 85% or equivalent
Must hold an active Cebuana Lhuiller Microsavings account
Applicant or their relatives must be a Cebuana Lhuiller client.
Priority Programs
● Accountancy or Financial Management
● Business-related courses
● Hotel and Restaurant Management or Hospitality Management
● Engineering
● Architecture
● Psychology
● Social Work
Requirements:
1. Proof of being a Cebuana Lhuillier client with a valid 24K Loyalty Card.
2. Proof of Financial Status.

● Engineering
● Architecture
● Psychology
● Social Work
Requirements:
1. Proof of being a Cebuana Lhuillier client with a valid 24K Loyalty Card.
2. Proof of Financial Status.
3. Copy of the latest grades showing a General Weighted Average (GWA)
4. Certificate of Good Moral Character and Certificate of Registration
5. Copy of university grading system.
Exam Information:
Exam Type: None
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://cebuanafoundation.org/

Charity First Educational Scholarship Program
Charity First Foundation
Overview
The Charity First Foundation, Inc. is a non-profit and non-stock organization founded by a group of
Chinese-Filipino businesspersons in 2001. The organization, evolving from giving natural disaster aids,
aims on improving the quality of life of the marginalized sectors.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strand
Coverage
Educational financial assistance per MOA
Eligibility
Priority Program: 1st or 2nd year STEM, Social Work, Education
Age: Must be 18 - 21 years old
Minimum GPA: 85% or equivalent
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Willingness to attend Charity First activities.
Priority Programs
● STEM
● Social Work
● Education
Requirements:
1. DSWD Case Study
2. Utility Bill with Address
3. Certified True Copy of Grades
4. Original and Photocopy Birth Certificate
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor

2. Utility Bill with Address
3. Certified True Copy of Grades
4. Original and Photocopy Birth Certificate
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor

Deltek Systems Scholarship
Deltek Systems (PHILIPPINES) LTD.
Overview
Deltek Systems (Philippines) Ltd. partners with the Polytechnic University of the Philippines (PUP) to
support 3rd year students enrolled in BS Computer Science and BS Computer Engineering.
Partial Coverage | Private | BSCS & BSCpE
Coverage
Php. 23,520.00 per semester
Eligibility
Priority Program: Bonafide 3rd year PUP student enrolled in BSCSor BS CpE
Minimum GPA: 2.50
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Nationality: Filipino
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 300,000
Not a working student
Priority Programs
● BS Computer Science
● BS Computer Engineering
Requirements:
1. Copy of Grades from 1st year to current (screenshot from SIS is accepted)
2. Current Registration Card
3. Parents’ or Guardian’s ITR, or Certificate of Indigency from Barangay
4. Personal Data Sheet / Application Form
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline

2. Current Registration Card
3. Parents’ or Guardian’s ITR, or Certificate of Indigency from Barangay
4. Personal Data Sheet / Application Form
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview

Moirasia Foundation Scholarship
Moirasia Foundation, Inc.
Overview
Moirasia Foundation, Inc. is the charitable branch of Ramon F. Garcia & Co., CPAs, focused on helping
students succeed. Through the Moirasia Youth Excellence Scholarship (MYES) program, the Foundation
provides financial support to students who need help continuing their education.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strands
Coverage
Educational financial assistance per MOA
Eligibility
Nationality: Filipino
Minimum GPA: 2.00
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 300,000
Must be physically and mentally healthy, subject for Moirasia pre-qualified physician
Priority Programs
● Open to all programs
Requirements:
1. Endorsement letter from dean or chairperson
2. PSA certificate of live birth
3. Barangay certificate of indigency
4. Certified copy of true grades and good moral character
5. DSWD case study report.
6. Autobiography

2. PSA certificate of live birth
3. Barangay certificate of indigency
4. Certified copy of true grades and good moral character
5. DSWD case study report.
6. Autobiography
7. Recommendation letters
8. Photocopy of valid school ID.
9. Printed photo of residence and sketch of address
10. Latest utility bills.
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Exam, Panel Interview, and Home/School visitation.
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.facebook.com/moirasiafoundation/

NITORI Philippines Scholarship
NITORI Philippines
Overview
The NITORI Philippines Scholarship provides financial assistance to eligible students, helping them
pursue their academic goals. It supports students by reducing financial barriers, allowing them to focus on
their studies and achieve success.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strands
Coverage
Php. 35,000.00 per year
Eligibility
Nationality: Filipino
Minimum GPA: 2.25
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 400,000
Academic Status: Must follow a regular curriculum, not a shiftee or irregular student.
Must be willing to attend NITORI Philippines events and events at the College of Arts and Letters.
Priority Programs
● College of Arts and Letters (CAL)
● College of Accountancy and Finance (CAF)
● College of Architecture, Design, and the Built Environment (CADBE)
● College of Business Administration (CBA)

● College of Arts and Letters (CAL)
● College of Accountancy and Finance (CAF)
● College of Architecture, Design, and the Built Environment (CADBE)
● College of Business Administration (CBA)
● College of Computer and Information Sciences (CCIS)
● College of Engineering (CE)
● College of Law (CL)
● College of Tourism, Hospitality, and Transportation Management (CTHTM)
Requirements:
● Personal Data Sheet or Application Form
● Copy of Grades
● Current Certificate of Registration
● Resume
● Parents’ or Guardian’s ITR or Certificate of Indigency.
Exam Information:
Exam Type: None
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor
More Information
https://www.nitori.com.ph/

Overseas Korean Traders Association Inc Scholarship
Overseas Korean Trading Association, Inc.
Overview
The Overseas Korean Traders Association Inc. Scholarship Program offers financial support to qualified
PUP students enrolled in any year level or course. With limited slots available, this program helps lessen
the financial burden through stipends and book allowances.
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strands
Coverage
Php. 7,425.00 per semester
Eligibility
Nationality: Filipino
Minimum GPA: 2.00
Scholarship Exclusivity: Must not be a recipient of any other scholarship program.
Financial Status: Must not exceed Php. 400,000
Academic Status: Must follow a regular curriculum, not a shiftee or irregular student.
Must not be a working student
Priority Programs
● Open to all programs
Requirements:
● Copy of Grades
● Latest Certificate of Registration
● Parent / Guardian ITR
● Personal Data Sheet
Exam Information:
Exam Type: Interview
Deadline
Subject to deadline set by the benefactor

● Copy of Grades
● Latest Certificate of Registration
● Parent / Guardian ITR
● Personal Data Sheet
Exam Information:

Panasonic College Scholarship
Overseas Korean Trading Association, Inc.
Overview
Panasonic College Scholarship offers financial assistance for a selected students enrolled in a state
college of university
Partial Coverage | Private | All Strands
Coverage
Educational financial assistance per MOA
Eligibility
● Must be a Filipino citizen
● Must be a full-time student enrolled in a state college or university
● Must be a college student
● Must be graduating within the next two years
● Minimum GPA: 2.00
Priority Programs
● Engineering
● Health Science
● Applied Science

DBP INSPIRE Scholarship Program
Development Bank of the Philippines
Overview
The DBP INSPIRE Scholarship Program stands for Inclusive Integrated Scholastic Program for Inclusive
and Responsive Education.This initiative of the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) reflects its
commitment to making education more accessible.
Partial Coverage | National Government | All Strand
Coverage
Full coverage of allowable academic fees, and monthly living allowance.
Eligibility
● Incoming first-year college student (freshman) or a higher-level college student from a
low-income family.
● Filipino of good moral character to be supported by a letter of recommendation from the school
last attended.
● Come from an indigent family with an annual net family income not exceeding ₱150,000.
● Have a General Weighted Average (GWA) of 85% or higher with no grade below 80%.
● Pass medical, dental, physical, and psychological examinations.

● Have a General Weighted Average (GWA) of 85% or higher with no grade below 80%.
● Pass medical, dental, physical, and psychological examinations.
● Not be a recipient of any other scholarship or study grant from other benefactors (except for Free
Higher Education privilege).
Priority Programs
● Open to All Programs

1. Application letter addressed to the University President (expressing willingness to follow DBP policies).

3. Latest Income Tax Return (ITR) or any valid proof of family income.
4. PSA/Local Civil Registry Birth Certificate.
5. Enrollment Form/Study Load (photocopy).
